validation_rules: require a Mathlib import probe status before any Mathlib availability claim
reject Mathlib-dependent consumer cases that skip the readiness gate
treat translation smoke rows as non-proof metadata
mark absent corpora as blocked capabilities
reject proof, provider, or oracle body fields
reject private source refs and release/proof-authority overclaims
emit secret-exclusion scans and provenance-bearing receipts over copied non-secret corpus readiness substrate
require exported bundles to carry a source_module_manifest.json with source_ref, target_ref, sha256, material_class, body_copied=true, and body_in_receipt=false for every copied macro body
fail exported bundle validation when a copied source artifact target is missing, digest drifted, uses an unsupported material class, or claims body_in_receipt=true
receipt_expectations: receipt must cite source pattern ids, real source refs, source digests, body material status, corpus/toolchain readiness status, corpus counts, blocked capabilities, consumer-gate decisions, negative-case coverage, secret-exclusion scan, authority ceiling, and anti-claim

allowed_public_inputs: public-safe real source refs and digests
copied non-secret corpus/toolchain source artifacts under exported bundle source_artifacts
source_module_manifest rows with exact source-to-target digest coupling
fixture negative cases as regression-only leakage guards

anti_claim: This standard governs copied non-secret corpus/toolchain readiness substrate from the 2026-05-11 proof-state curriculum smoke run. It does not run Lean/Lake, claim Mathlib availability beyond the recorded failed probe, prove theorem correctness, benchmark formal-math corpora, expose proof bodies, call providers, or authorize release.

standard_payload.ontology: A provenance-bearing formal-math corpus readiness gate that carries copied non-secret PROVER smoke-run corpus/toolchain bodies and makes the recorded Lean/Std and Mathlib absence boundary explicit before downstream retrieval, tactic routing, or proof-witness work.
standard_payload.teleology: Let Microcosm carry the real non-secret corpus readiness and tactic-affordance boundary bodies instead of replacing the macro run with synthetic environment metadata or source pointers.
standard_payload.anti_purpose: Turning a readiness fixture, source ref, or copied body into proof authority, corpus benchmark authority, or release authority.
failure_modes: Mathlib availability is claimed without a passing probe
translation smoke rows imply proof authority
absent corpus is treated as usable
documentation claims release or proof authority
source_module_manifest missing while body_material_status claims copied corpus/toolchain body
copied corpus/toolchain source artifact digest drift is ignored

omission_receipt.reason: public slice carries copied non-secret corpus readiness rows, real source refs, source digests, target refs, negative leakage guards, and secret-exclusion receipts; proof bodies, provider payloads, raw Mathlib source bodies, private logs, and release claims remain excluded

anti_claim_contract.rule: Corpus readiness material must state that it is not Lean/Lake execution, Mathlib proof authority, benchmark authority, provider authority, or release authority

source_coupling_contract.rule: macro source refs and source digests remain public-root relative where applicable; copied non-secret corpus readiness rows retain provenance, while proof/provider/private bodies and raw Mathlib source bodies remain excluded
